note: Working chronology for narrative structure. Pre-1990 dates are approximate and must be locked to primary sources before script lock; entries here that drive factual claims are tied to claim IDs.

events.event: Bernard L. Madoff founds Bernard L. Madoff Investment Securities (a legitimate broker-dealer/market-making business that coexisted with the later-fraudulent advisory arm).

The investment advisory arm is, by the trustee's findings, operating as a Ponzi scheme: client money is not really invested; 'returns' are paid from other investors' principal; account statements are fabricated.
Analyst Harry Markopolos concludes Madoff's steady returns are mathematically impossible and begins warning the SEC.
Markopolos submits a detailed memo to the SEC (commonly referred to as 'The World's Largest Hedge Fund Is a Fraud' — title to be primary-verified).
Madoff is arrested after confessing to his sons during the financial crisis, when redemption demands he cannot meet expose the scheme.
Madoff pleads guilty to an 11-count criminal information and is remanded into custody.
Madoff is sentenced to 150 years in federal prison.
SEC Office of Inspector General report examines the agency's failure to uncover the scheme despite repeated warnings.
Madoff dies in federal prison at age 82, having served ~12 years.
SIPA trustee Irving Picard and the DOJ Madoff Victim Fund recover the large majority of lost principal (trustee recoveries ~$14.7-15.3B by mid-2025; many victims recover >90% of actual losses).
